The plaintiff is seeking to implead additional rogue-websites and their domain name registrars as defendant nos. 22 to 158 because the websites have been found to be indulging in illegally disseminating the copyrighted material belonging to the plaintiff. 2. Proposed defendant nos. 22 to 150 are rogue websites and the defendant no.151 to 158 are DNR of proposed defendants. The application is supported by the required affidavit. Keeping in view the contents of the suit and the application, the same is allowed. 3. Ld counsel for plaintiff submits that they are not pressing for reliefs qua proposed defendant no.48 and 155 and they would like This is a digitally signed order. In view of submissions made by Ld counsel for plaintiff, proposed defendant no.48 and 155 stands deleted from the array of parties. 4. Let fresh amended memo of parties be filed within one week. Steps be taken to serve the newly impleaded defendants (defendant nos.22 to 47, 49 to 154 and 156 to 158) with summons within two weeks, through the electronic mode only, on filing of PF. 5. The order blocking the rogue-websites and injunction order passed by the Hon’ble Court on 30.01.2026 shall operate qua the freshly impleaded defendants as well. Further, the concerned DNR’s are directed to lock and suspend / deactivate the rogue websites impleaded today. The concerned ISPs /TSPs are directed to block the rogue websites impleaded today.
